summary:
The main purpose of a dentin conditioner is to enhance dental bond strength through changes in the chemical and physical nature of the dentinal tubules’ walls; this process improves adhesive retention when applying composite resins or other filling materials directly onto prepared teeth structures without causing harm during routine procedures such as cavity fillings. To achieve optimal results, it prepares an ideal interface between natural tissue and artificial restoration components via increased wetting capacity facilitated primarily due to alterations made upon application over exposed root structure areas prone to demineralization processes leading towards caries formation if left untreated properly under professional guidance conditions provided within standard operating protocols established across various healthcare settings globally today.
 In summary:

Dentin conditioners increase surface energy to promote stronger bonds with restoratives, enhancing attachment while preserving healthy tooth structure. They prepare the dentine for effective sealing against decay by improving its interaction with applied materials. Sentence count: 2
